terms.definition: Route of administration via the mouth, swallowed and absorbed through the gastrointestinal tract.
terms.exampleSentence: Take this medicine by the oral route, with a full glass of water and food.
terms.contextNotes: Use 'by mouth' on patient labels rather than 'PO'.

terms.definition: Administration directly into a vein for rapid systemic effect; requires aseptic technique.
terms.exampleSentence: Furosemide 40 mg was given by the intravenous route to relieve acute pulmonary oedema.
terms.contextNotes: Check compatibility before mixing IV drugs in one line.

terms.definition: Injection deep into a muscle (deltoid, gluteus, vastus lateralis); slower absorption than IV.
terms.exampleSentence: The flu vaccine is given by the intramuscular route into the deltoid.
terms.contextNotes: Z-track technique reduces leakage of irritant drugs.

terms.definition: Injection into the fatty tissue beneath the skin; used for insulin, heparin and many biologics.
terms.exampleSentence: Insulin is administered by the subcutaneous route, usually into the abdomen or thigh.
terms.contextNotes: Rotate injection sites to prevent lipohypertrophy with insulin.

terms.definition: Injection between the layers of the skin producing a small bleb; used for Mantoux and allergy testing.
terms.exampleSentence: The tuberculin test is performed by the intradermal route on the volar forearm.
terms.contextNotes: Volume is typically only 0.1 mL.

terms.definition: Applied to the surface of the skin or mucous membrane for local effect (creams, ointments, gels).
terms.exampleSentence: Apply the topical hydrocortisone 1% cream sparingly twice a day for no more than seven days.
terms.contextNotes: Quantify steroid use with the fingertip unit (FTU).

terms.definition: Administration under the tongue for rapid mucosal absorption (e.g. GTN spray, buprenorphine).
terms.exampleSentence: Place one GTN tablet under your tongue at the first sign of chest pain.
terms.contextNotes: Avoid swallowing to preserve first-pass-bypassing absorption.

terms.definition: Placed between the cheek and gum for mucosal absorption (e.g. midazolam, prochlorperazine).
terms.exampleSentence: Buccal midazolam is used as a rescue medication for prolonged seizures in the community.
terms.contextNotes: Patient must not chew or swallow the tablet.

terms.definition: Administration into the rectum (suppository, enema, foam); useful when oral route unavailable.
terms.exampleSentence: Diazepam was given by the rectal route during the convulsion as IV access was unavailable.
terms.contextNotes: Counsel on insertion direction (pointed end first).

terms.definition: Drug breathed into the airways for direct pulmonary effect; minimises systemic side effects.
terms.exampleSentence: Inhaled corticosteroids are the cornerstone of preventer therapy in asthma.
terms.contextNotes: Always check inhaler technique at every review.

terms.definition: Drug delivered as a fine aerosol mist via a nebuliser, typically driven by oxygen or air.
terms.exampleSentence: She received nebulised salbutamol 5 mg back-to-back during the acute asthma exacerbation.
terms.contextNotes: Drive with air (not oxygen) in COPD with type 2 respiratory failure.

terms.definition: Administered to the eye as drops or ointment; preservative-free options for chronic use.
terms.exampleSentence: Pull down the lower lid and instil one ophthalmic drop into the conjunctival sac.
terms.contextNotes: Press on the inner canthus for one minute to limit systemic absorption.

terms.definition: Administered to the ear, typically as drops; warm bottle to body temperature before use.
terms.exampleSentence: Instil two otic drops into the affected ear and lie on your side for five minutes.
terms.contextNotes: Avoid aminoglycoside drops if the eardrum is perforated.

terms.definition: Adhesive patch delivering drug across intact skin at a controlled rate (e.g. fentanyl, HRT).
terms.exampleSentence: Apply the transdermal fentanyl patch to a hairless area of upper trunk and rotate sites.
terms.contextNotes: Remove old patch before applying new one; document site.

terms.definition: Long-acting parenteral formulation slowly released from an oily vehicle (e.g. depot antipsychotics).
terms.exampleSentence: Paliperidone, a depot injection, is given monthly into the gluteal or deltoid muscle.
terms.contextNotes: Wait 2-3 weeks after first dose to assess steady state.

terms.definition: Metered-dose inhaler; pressurised canister delivering a measured aerosol dose per actuation.
terms.exampleSentence: Demonstrate slow, deep inhalation with the MDI and breath-holding for ten seconds.
terms.contextNotes: Always recommend a spacer when possible.

terms.definition: Dry powder inhaler; breath-actuated, requires fast forceful inhalation (no propellant).
terms.exampleSentence: A DPI such as the Turbohaler requires a fast, forceful breath in to release the dose.
terms.contextNotes: Not suitable when inspiratory flow is poor.

terms.definition: Plastic chamber attached to a metered-dose inhaler that improves drug delivery to the lungs.
terms.exampleSentence: Use the AeroChamber spacer with your blue inhaler — it improves delivery to your airways.
terms.contextNotes: Wash monthly in detergent; air-dry without towel-drying.

terms.definition: Device that converts liquid medication into a fine mist for inhalation, driven by gas or ultrasound.
terms.exampleSentence: Set the nebuliser at 6-8 L/min flow rate and continue for around ten minutes.
terms.contextNotes: Driving gas matters: air for COPD with type 2 respiratory failure.

terms.definition: Single intravenous dose given over a short period (typically minutes) for rapid effect.
terms.exampleSentence: Adenosine 6 mg was given as a rapid IV bolus followed by a 20 mL saline flush.
terms.contextNotes: Always specify the rate of administration.

terms.definition: Continuous or intermittent intravenous administration of fluid/drug over a defined time.
terms.exampleSentence: Vancomycin 1 g was given as an IV infusion over at least 100 minutes.
terms.contextNotes: Confirm rate, diluent, compatibility and line.

terms.definition: Battery-powered pump delivering a continuous subcutaneous infusion, often used in palliative care.
terms.exampleSentence: We started a syringe driver with morphine and midazolam for end-of-life symptom control.
terms.contextNotes: Check site daily for irritation; document drugs and doses.

terms.definition: Percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy tube placed through the abdominal wall into the stomach.
terms.exampleSentence: Crush the tablets and flush with water before administering via the PEG tube.
terms.contextNotes: Liquid or dispersible formulations preferred where available.

terms.definition: Patient-controlled analgesia device allowing the patient to self-administer set boluses of opioid.
terms.exampleSentence: Press the PCA pump button when you feel pain — it has a five-minute lockout.
terms.contextNotes: Only the patient should press the button — never relatives.

terms.definition: Drug administered into the epidural space surrounding the spinal cord; used for analgesia or anaesthesia.
terms.exampleSentence: An epidural infusion of bupivacaine and fentanyl was set up for postoperative pain.
terms.contextNotes: Monitor BP, motor block and infection risk closely.
